final_answer:
Based on my comprehensive analysis of sample d3504a6d-3e1d-47a0-87cf-09191452c6ac, I can confidently provide the final structure determination.

## Summary of Evidence

| Data Type | Result | Interpretation |
|-----------|--------|----------------|
| **Molecular Weight** | 226.275 Da | Consistent with C₁₅H₁₄O₂ |
| **Molecular Formula** | C₁₅H₁₄O₂ | DBE = 9 (two aromatic rings + carbonyl) |
| **¹H NMR** | δ 8.11 (d, 2H), 7.65 (d, 2H), 7.62 (d, 2H), 7.46 (t, 2H), 7.39 (t, 1H), 4.40 (q, 2H), 1.41 (t, 3H) | Para-disubstituted benzene + monosubstituted phenyl + ethyl ester |
| **¹³C NMR** | δ 166.3, 145.4, 139.9, 130.0, 129.3, 128.8, 128.0, 127.1, 126.9, 60.8, 14.2 | Ester carbonyl, aromatic carbons, ethyl group |

## Structure Identification

The compound is **Ethyl 4-phenylbenzoate** (also known as ethyl biphenyl-4-carboxylate):

```
        O
        ‖
    C—O—CH₂—CH₃
    |
[benzene ring]—[benzene ring]
```

FINAL_RESULT:
  UUID: d3504a6d-3e1d-47a0-87cf-09191452c6ac
  PREDICTED_SMILES: CCOC(=O)c1ccc(-c2ccccc2)cc1
  CONFIDENCE: 0.95
  REASON_BRIEF: The molecular formula C₁₅H₁₄O₂ (MW 226.27, DBE=9) combined with ¹H NMR showing a para-disubstituted benzene ring with characteristic downfield protons ortho to ester (δ 8.11), a monosubstituted phenyl ring (δ 7.62, 7.46, 7.39), and an ethyl ester group (quartet at δ 4.40, triplet at δ 1.41), along with ¹³C NMR confirming an ester carbonyl at δ 166.3, unambiguously identifies this compound as ethyl 4-phenylbenzoate.

steps.content:
You are a senior expert in organic synthesis and spectroscopic structure elucidation, working in a laboratory to analyze an unknown small organic molecule with ID d3504a6d-3e1d-47a0-87cf-09191452c6ac. You may call tools to obtain MS, 1H NMR, 13C NMR, and other data, and then infer its structure.
[Your Objectives] 1. Use all available spectroscopic and molecular information to elucidate the structure. 2. When information is insufficient, plan and call only the necessary tools to collect evidence. 3. Provide the most plausible candidate structure (as SMILES) and a confidence score. 4. Efficiency matters (important): each tool call simulates a real experiment with time and monetary cost.
   Prioritize correctness of the final structure, but minimize the number of tool calls by reasoning deeply
   from the data already available.

[Available Tools] The tool list below is injected at runtime and always matches the tools you can actually call:
- Measure_MW(uuid): Get molecular weight. Returns a float in Da.
- Measure_Formula(uuid): Get molecular formula. Returns a string, e.g. "C22H32N2O3S".
- Get_13C_NMR(uuid): Get 13C NMR peak list/description text. Returns a string, e.g. "13C NMR (75 MHz, CDCl3): δ ...".
- Calculate_DBE(uuid): Calculate degrees of unsaturation (DBE). Requires sample SMILES and RDKit. Returns a float; returns -1.0 if unavailable (unknown).
- Get_1H_NMR(uuid): Get 1H NMR peak list/description text. Returns a string, e.g. "1H NMR (300 MHz, CDCl3): δ ...".
- Check_Data(uuid): List which experimental data are available for the sample. Returns a list of tool names, e.g. ["Measure_MW", "Get_13C_NMR", "Get_IR"].
- Get_IR(uuid): Get IR spectrum description text. Commonly used to confirm functional groups (e.g., C=O, O-H).
- Get_19F_NMR(uuid): Get 19F NMR description text. Typically meaningful only when the molecule contains F.
- Get_31P_NMR(uuid): Get 31P NMR description text. Typically meaningful only when the molecule contains P.
- Get_HRMS(uuid): Get HRMS description text/peak info. Commonly used to confirm molecular formula, isotopic pattern, or exact mass.
- Get_MS(uuid): Get MS (non-HRMS) description text/peak info. Commonly used to estimate molecular weight and inspect fragment peaks.
- Get_Melting_Point(uuid): Get melting point information. Returns a string (typically includes unit and range, e.g. "mp 120–122 °C").
- Get_TLC(uuid): Get TLC information (Rf, eluent/solvent system, etc.). Returns a string.
- Get_Optical_Rotation(uuid): Get optical rotation information (e.g., [α]D value, solvent, concentration). Returns a string.
[System Behavior Note (important)] - To avoid invalid calls: if you do not call any tool at the very beginning, the system may automatically
  run Check_Data once to confirm which data are available for the current sample. Unless you truly need to
  verify availability, you do not have to treat Check_Data as a mandatory first step.

[Suggested Reasoning Workflow (adjust as needed)] 1. Strategy planning: identify the most critical uncertainty first; avoid blindly requesting all spectra.
   If you are unsure whether certain data exist or want to avoid wasted calls, you may call Check_Data.
2. Iterative data acquisition (important): acquire data step-by-step. After each tool result, pause to
   reason and decide whether you are still uncertain. Only then request the next most informative test.
   Prefer calling at most ONE new tool per iteration unless multiple calls are strictly necessary.
3. Basic data: call Measure_MW and Measure_Formula to obtain molecular weight and molecular formula. 4. First pass: use Calculate_DBE to estimate unsaturation and form initial hypotheses. 5. Core elucidation: prioritize Get_1H_NMR (and Get_13C_NMR only if needed). Use chemical shifts,
   integrals, splitting patterns, and carbon environments to build one or more plausible scaffolds.
6. Targeted validation: only when ambiguity remains, cautiously call Get_IR / Get_HRMS (etc.) to obtain
   specific discriminating evidence.
7. Once you have the most plausible candidate structure, provide its SMILES. 8. If the evidence is still clearly insufficient for a unique structure, explicitly state what is uncertain,
   and provide your best current candidate with an appropriate confidence.

[Output Requirements (very important)] When you believe you are done and no further tool calls are needed, your final reply must include the following structured block (key names must match exactly). You may provide natural-language reasoning before it, but the block below is mandatory:
FINAL_RESULT:
  UUID: d3504a6d-3e1d-47a0-87cf-09191452c6ac
  PREDICTED_SMILES: <SMILES of your best candidate; if you cannot give one, write "UNKNOWN">
  CONFIDENCE: <a decimal between 0 and 1, e.g., 0.8>
  REASON_BRIEF: <1–3 sentences summarizing the key evidence supporting your choice>

Notes: - If multiple candidates are plausible, you must output only your top choice in PREDICTED_SMILES;
  you may mention other candidates in the free-text reasoning.
- If you believe the data are insufficient for a reliable structure, you must set PREDICTED_SMILES="UNKNOWN"
  and explain why in REASON_BRIEF.

The unique identifier of the current sample is d3504a6d-3e1d-47a0-87cf-09191452c6ac. Start by planning which tools to call, then proceed step by step to complete the structure elucidation.

I'll start analyzing sample d3504a6d-3e1d-47a0-87cf-09191452c6ac by first gathering the fundamental molecular information. Let me get the molecular weight and molecular formula, which are essential starting points for structure elucidation.

Excellent! I have the basic molecular information:
- **Molecular Weight**: 226.275 Da
- **Molecular Formula**: C₁₅H₁₄O₂

Let me calculate the degree of unsaturation (DBE) to understand the unsaturation level:

DBE = (2×15 + 2 - 14) / 2 = (30 + 2 - 14) / 2 = 18/2 = **9**

A DBE of 9 is quite high, suggesting:
- Likely an aromatic system (a benzene ring = 4 DBE)
- With 9 DBE, this could be two benzene rings (8 DBE) + 1 additional unsaturation, or a fused aromatic system like naphthalene (7 DBE) + additional features

The formula C₁₅H₁₄O₂ with 9 DBE suggests a biphenyl or diphenyl system with oxygen-containing functional groups. Let me get the ¹H NMR to understand the structure better.

This ¹H NMR is very informative! Let me analyze:

1. **δ 8.11 (d, J = 8.6 Hz, 2H)** - Aromatic protons, likely ortho to electron-withdrawing group (ester carbonyl)
2. **δ 7.65 (d, J = 8.6 Hz, 2H)** - Aromatic protons, para-substituted benzene ring
3. **δ 7.62 (d, J = 7.5 Hz, 2H)** - Aromatic protons, ortho positions of monosubstituted phenyl
4. **δ 7.46 (t, J = 7.5 Hz, 2H)** - Aromatic protons, meta positions of monosubstituted phenyl
5. **δ 7.39 (t, J = 7.4 Hz, 1H)** - Aromatic proton, para position of monosubstituted phenyl
6. **δ 4.40 (q, J = 7.1 Hz, 2H)** - Ethyl ester -OCH₂- (quartet)
7. **δ 1.41 (t, J = 7.2 Hz, 3H)** - Ethyl ester -CH₃ (triplet)

**Structure Analysis:**
- The pattern shows a **para-disubstituted benzene ring** (2H + 2H with J = 8.6 Hz)
- A **monosubstituted phenyl ring** (2H + 2H + 1H pattern typical of Ph-)
- An **ethyl ester group** (-CO₂CH₂CH₃)

This strongly suggests **ethyl 4-phenylbenzoate** (ethyl biphenyl-4-carboxylate):
- Biphenyl system (C₁₂H₉) accounts for 8 DBE
- Ester carbonyl (C=O) accounts for 1 DBE
- Total: 9 DBE ✓

The downfield shift at δ 8.11 is characteristic of aromatic protons ortho to an ester group, and δ 7.65 would be the protons adjacent to the phenyl substituent.

Let me verify with ¹³C NMR to confirm:

The ¹³C NMR confirms the structure:

1. **δ 166.3** - Ester carbonyl carbon (C=O)
2. **δ 145.4** - Quaternary aromatic carbon (C-4 of benzoate ring, attached to phenyl)
3. **δ 139.9** - Quaternary aromatic carbon (C-1 of phenyl ring, attached to benzoate)
4. **δ 130.0** - Aromatic CH (ortho to ester)
5. **δ 129.3** - Quaternary aromatic carbon (C-1 of benzoate, attached to ester)
6. **δ 128.8, 128.0, 127.1, 126.9** - Aromatic CH carbons
7. **δ 60.8** - Ester -OCH₂- carbon
8. **δ 14.2** - Ester -CH₃ carbon

This is fully consistent with **ethyl 4-phenylbenzoate** (ethyl biphenyl-4-carboxylate).

The structure is:
- A biphenyl core with an ethyl ester at the 4-position
- SMILES: **CCOC(=O)c1ccc(-c2ccccc2)cc1**

All data are consistent:
- MW: 226.27 ✓
- Formula: C₁₅H₁₄O₂ ✓
- DBE: 9 ✓
- ¹H NMR: para-substituted + monosubstituted phenyl + ethyl ester ✓
- ¹³C NMR: 11 signals matching the expected carbons ✓

FINAL_RESULT:
  UUID: d3504a6d-3e1d-47a0-87cf-09191452c6ac
  PREDICTED_SMILES: CCOC(=O)c1ccc(-c2ccccc2)cc1
  CONFIDENCE: 0.95
  REASON_BRIEF: The molecular formula C₁₅H₁₄O₂ (MW 226.27, DBE=9) combined with ¹H NMR showing a para-disubstituted benzene (δ 8.11, 7.65), a monosubstituted phenyl (δ 7.62, 7.46, 7.39), and an ethyl ester (δ 4.40 q, 1.41 t), along with ¹³C NMR confirming an ester carbonyl (δ 166.3) and appropriate aromatic/aliphatic carbons, strongly supports ethyl 4-phenylbenzoate (ethyl biphenyl-4-carboxylate).

Based on my comprehensive analysis of sample d3504a6d-3e1d-47a0-87cf-09191452c6ac, I can confidently provide the final structure determination.

## Summary of Evidence

| Data Type | Result | Interpretation |
|-----------|--------|----------------|
| **Molecular Weight** | 226.275 Da | Consistent with C₁₅H₁₄O₂ |
| **Molecular Formula** | C₁₅H₁₄O₂ | DBE = 9 (two aromatic rings + carbonyl) |
| **¹H NMR** | δ 8.11 (d, 2H), 7.65 (d, 2H), 7.62 (d, 2H), 7.46 (t, 2H), 7.39 (t, 1H), 4.40 (q, 2H), 1.41 (t, 3H) | Para-disubstituted benzene + monosubstituted phenyl + ethyl ester |
| **¹³C NMR** | δ 166.3, 145.4, 139.9, 130.0, 129.3, 128.8, 128.0, 127.1, 126.9, 60.8, 14.2 | Ester carbonyl, aromatic carbons, ethyl group |

## Structure Identification

The compound is **Ethyl 4-phenylbenzoate** (also known as ethyl biphenyl-4-carboxylate):

```
        O
        ‖
    C—O—CH₂—CH₃
    |
[benzene ring]—[benzene ring]
```

  PREDICTED_SMILES: CCOC(=O)c1ccc(-c2ccccc2)cc1
  CONFIDENCE: 0.95
  REASON_BRIEF: The molecular formula C₁₅H₁₄O₂ (MW 226.27, DBE=9) combined with ¹H NMR showing a para-disubstituted benzene ring with characteristic downfield protons ortho to ester (δ 8.11), a monosubstituted phenyl ring (δ 7.62, 7.46, 7.39), and an ethyl ester group (quartet at δ 4.40, triplet at δ 1.41), along with ¹³C NMR confirming an ester carbonyl at δ 166.3, unambiguously identifies this compound as ethyl 4-phenylbenzoate.
